skill.name: video-processor
skill.description: Process video files with audio extraction, format conversion (mp4, webm), and Whisper transcription. Use when user mentions video conversion, audio extraction, transcription, mp4, webm, ffmpeg, or whisper transcription.
skill.summary: Convert videos, extract audio, and create transcripts with FFmpeg and Whisper.
skill.author: EGAdams

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly found external command execution and local filesystem access, but I did not find shell execution, network access, credential access, or prompt injection. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives; no cryptographic algorithm use was found. This skill is publishable with warnings because it intentionally processes user media through FFmpeg and Whisper.

medium_findings.title: External Media Tool Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The script invokes FFmpeg and Whisper through subprocess and ffmpeg-python for dependency checks, conversion, extraction, and transcription. Calls use argument lists and fixed executable names, so I did not find shell injection, but processing untrusted media through native tools remains elevated risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external command calls are directly present and central to the skill. Confidence is not higher because the implementation avoids shell=True and uses structured arguments.

low_findings.title: Local File Creation and Cleanup
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with limited scope. The transcription workflow creates a temporary WAV file and removes it with os.unlink during errors and cleanup. This is expected for media processing, but it requires local filesystem access and best-effort cleanup can leave temporary files if the process is interrupted.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The temporary file and deletion logic is explicit and easy to verify. The risk is low because the deleted path is created by the script.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but I found no hash, encryption, or password storage logic in the cited files. The cited lines are descriptions, media format documentation, or transcript output handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Search and manual review found no cryptographic API or weak hash usage. The cited locations discuss media processing behavior, not cryptography.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Executable Command Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for malicious shell behavior. SKILL.md includes many bash examples that instruct users to run the local video_processor.py script, install FFmpeg, and install Whisper. These are expected setup and usage examples, with no evidence of obfuscation, exfiltration, or destructive commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible and match the stated purpose of the skill. They still deserve a low-risk note because users may execute local media tools.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Used for Tool Checks and Transcription
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses subprocess.run to call ffmpeg and whisper. The pattern is potentially dangerous in general, but these calls use list arguments and no shell expansion.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are direct evidence of external command execution. The implementation reduces injection risk by avoiding shell strings.

dangerous_patterns.title: FFmpeg Runs on User-Selected Media Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill passes user-selected input and output paths to ffmpeg-python for audio extraction and video conversion. This is normal for the skill, but native media parsers have higher exposure when used on untrusted files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The ffmpeg processing calls are clearly present and user-selected files are part of the CLI design. The concern is contextual rather than evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Video conversion and transcription often require several command-line tools and careful output settings. This skill provides a focused workflow for FFmpeg conversion, audio extraction, and Whisper transcription.
actual_capabilities: Extract audio from video files into WAV, MP3, AAC, or FLAC formats.
Convert supported video files to MP4 with codec, preset, and CRF options.
Convert supported video files to WebM with VP8 or VP9 settings.
Transcribe audio or video files with Whisper model choices from tiny to large.
Create transcript outputs in text, SRT, VTT, or JSON formats.
Use temporary WAV extraction for video transcription and clean up temporary files.
limitations: Requires FFmpeg and OpenAI Whisper to be installed in the runtime environment.
Large Whisper models can require significant memory and processing time.
Transcript accuracy depends on audio quality, language, accents, and background noise.
The tool processes local files only and does not provide cloud storage or sharing.

use_cases.title: Prepare Course Videos
use_cases.description: Convert lecture recordings to web formats and create transcript or subtitle files for review.

use_cases.title: Archive Meeting Recordings
use_cases.description: Extract audio from recorded sessions and generate searchable text transcripts.

use_cases.title: Publish Web Media
use_cases.description: Convert source videos to MP4 or WebM and create caption files for web publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the audio from this video into a WAV file and tell me where the output was saved.

prompt_templates.title: Convert to MP4
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this video to MP4 using a balanced quality setting suitable for general playback.

prompt_templates.title: Create Subtitles
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe this video with Whisper and create an SRT subtitle file using the small model.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Web Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a WebM version of this presentation, generate captions, and summarize the files produced.

output_examples.input: Convert an old camera AVI file to MP4.
output_examples.output: The skill produces an MP4 file with the requested name and reports the final file size.

output_examples.input: Transcribe a lecture video into subtitles.
output_examples.output: The skill extracts temporary audio, runs Whisper, saves an SRT subtitle file, and removes the temporary audio.

output_examples.input: Prepare a presentation video for a website.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a WebM video and a caption file that can be used during web publishing.

best_practices: Run the skill only on media files from trusted or reviewed sources.
Use smaller Whisper models for drafts and larger models for final review.
Check generated transcripts before publishing captions or written records.
anti_patterns: Do not process unknown media files in a privileged system environment.
Do not assume Whisper output is accurate enough for legal or medical records without review.
Do not overwrite original media files with converted outputs.

faq.question: Does this skill require FFmpeg?
faq.answer: Yes. FFmpeg must be installed before conversion, extraction, or video transcription workflows can run.

faq.question: Does this skill require Whisper?
faq.answer: Whisper is required for transcription. Conversion and audio extraction use FFmpeg.

faq.question: Can it create subtitle files?
faq.answer: Yes. The transcription command supports SRT and VTT outputs for captions.

faq.question: Can it process audio files directly?
faq.answer: Yes. Supported audio inputs include WAV, MP3, AAC, FLAC, M4A, and OGG.

faq.question: Does it upload files to an external service?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files do not contain network requests or upload logic.

faq.question: Is it safe to use on sensitive recordings?
faq.answer: Use caution. Processing is local, but transcripts and output files may contain sensitive information.
